seed.goal: This repository has no automated tests. As the Solution Architect, scope it; then the team must actually IMPLEMENT it: stand up a lightweight test runner (node --test via tsx, wired as the `test` npm script) and write meaningful unit tests for the highest-value untested pure logic — start with src/lib/format.ts (currency/percent/number formatting, including the signed-currency minus-sign and rounding behavior) and any ranking-delta or map-pack computation in src/features/rankings. Cover real edge cases (negative, zero, NaN, very large values), not happy-path smoke tests. If a test reveals a real defect, fix it. Keep the suite green and fast. The implementation/engineer role writes the code; do not stop at a plan.
seed.acceptance_hint: A real `test` script wired into package.json plus substantive unit tests on the highest-value pure logic, suite green. This is the 6-role-team validation (Dev Clone implementer added) of the run-10 gap where a 5-role team only planned. Judge: did the engineer actually write + run tests this time?
seed.notes: Validation seed for the 6-role SDLC team (architect→engineer/dev-clone→reviewer→security→release→docs). Targets the new team by id. Same goal as local-seo/test-coverage (run-10, NOT-READY) to confirm the implementer gap is resolved.
